images.caption:
Figure 1:Replay pairing. The same trajectory is run twice: A keeps the plan in history; B
replays the identical actions and observations with the plan removed. The plan-fidelity
signal is the per-step cosine distance between the two hidden states. It spikes one step
after the plan and fades to near zero within a step: a plan re-read from context, not stored
in hidden state.

Figure 3:Plan signal (mean±\pmSE, 80 tasks) as a function of step offset from guard injection. Pre-plan signal (s<0s{<}0) is near zero (validation). Signal jumps to0.4530.453at step+1+1then drops4.1×4.1\timesin one step.

header: 8When Context Management Fails: A Compression Stress Test

images.caption: Figure 4:Context-compression stress test on 30 ALFWorld tasks and 5 runs per task. Naive eviction sharply reduces task success, while two plan-aware policies do not recover performance at the same context budget. Error bars show standard errors.

Figure 5:Step-index leakage atL8L_{8}. Real binary AUROC (1.0001.000) drops to chance (0.2970.297) under label shuffling. The Ridge regression target atL32L_{32}(continuous plan-signal magnitude) sidesteps this confound.

Figure 6:Reliability diagram for theL32L_{32}probe. Diagonal
indicates perfect calibration.

Figure 7:Multi-model panel. Llama-3.1-70B: full replay-paired decay curve, peakL32L_{32}, decay4.1×4.1\times. Llama-3.1-8B: same shape at smaller scale (peak0.3820.382,10.9×10.9\timesdecay) at matched relative depth around33%33\%. Phi-3 and Qwen2.5: plan signal not computable from archival data (non-replay-paired).
